title: Virtual Private Cloud (VPC)
summary: A VPC is your private network boundary in the cloud: carve CIDR space, split public vs private subnets, and route traffic deliberately instead of exposing every resource to the internet.

title: Public vs Private Subnets
summary: Public subnets talk to the internet via an IGW; private subnets reach out through NAT (or not at all). Workloads that do not need inbound internet belong in private space.

title: Load Balancer (L4 vs L7)
summary: L4 balances TCP/UDP streams; L7 (HTTP) can route by host/path and terminate TLS. Pick the layer that matches what your app understands.

title: Object Storage (S3)
summary: Object storage is flat buckets + keys — great for assets, backups, and data lakes. Lock down access with IAM and bucket policies, not public ACLs.

title: Queue (SQS)
summary: Queues buffer work between producers and consumers, absorb spikes, and let you scale workers independently — pair with a DLQ so poison messages do not block the line forever.

title: Serverless Functions (Lambda)
summary: Functions run on managed compute in response to events. Keep handlers small, idempotent, and load secrets from a vault — never bake credentials into the bundle.
